гҖңгӮүгҒ—гҒ„ can mean "seems" or describe a typical characteristic.
гҖңгҒ§гҒҷ/гҖңгҒҫгҒҷ is the polite sentence ending used in formal conversation.
гҖңгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“ is the polite negative form, used to say something does not happen.
гҖңгҒҹгҒ„ attaches to a verb stem to express wanting to do something.
гҖңгҒҰгӮӮгҒ„гҒ„гҒ§гҒҷ means it is okay to do something; it grants permission.
гҖңгҒҰгҒҜгҒ„гҒ‘гҒҫгҒӣгӮ“ means you must not do something; it forbids an action.
гҖңгҒӘгҒҢгӮү connects actions happening at the same time.
гҖңгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ expresses past experience, like have done something before.
гҖңгҒҰгҒ—гҒҫгҒҶ can show completion or regret about an action.
гҖңгҒқгҒҶгҒ§гҒҷ reports hearsay, like "I heard that...".
гҖңгӮҲгҒҶгҒ§гҒҷ means it seems like and is based on observation.
гҖңгҒ§гҒ—гӮҮгҒҶ adds probability or softens statements, like probably.
гҖңгҒӨгӮӮгӮҠ expresses intent, like I plan to do something.
гҖңгҒ§гҒҷ/гҖңгҒҫгҒҷ for polite statements; it's the standard polite ending for everyday conversation.
гҖңгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“ is the polite negative form of verbs, used to say something doesn't happen.
"гҖңгҒҹгҒ„" attaches to a verb stem to express "want to do" something.
гҖңгҒҰгҒ—гҒҫгҒҶ can show completion or regret about an action.
гҖңгҒҰгҒ—гҒҫгҒҶ can show completion or regret about an action.
"гҖңгҒқгҒҶгҒ§гҒҷ" can report hearsay ("I heard that...").
"гҖңгӮҲгҒҶгҒ§гҒҷ" means "it seems like" and is based on observation.
"гҖңгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ" expresses past experience, like "have done" something before.
"гҖңгҒӨгӮӮгӮҠ" expresses intent, like "I plan to" do something.
"гҖңгҒҰгӮӮгӮүгҒҶ" means "receive a favor" from someone.
"гҖңгҒҰгҒӮгҒ’гӮӢ" shows the speaker does a favor for someone else.
"гҖңгӮҲгҒҶгҒЁжҖқгҒҶ" expresses a decision made at the moment of speaking.
"гҖңгҒӘгӮү" means "if" or "in the case of" and often gives advice.
"гҖңгҒҹгӮү" is a conditional "if/when" that also expresses sequence.
"гҖңгҒҫгҒҫ" means "as is" or "unchanged".

гҖңгҒӨгӮӮгӮҠ expresses intent, like "I plan to" do something.
гҖңгҒҰгӮӮгӮүгҒҶ means "receive a favor" from someone.
гҖңгҒҷгҒҺгӮӢ attaches to verbs or adjectives to mean "too much".
гҖңгҒҰгҒӮгҒ’гӮӢ shows the speaker does a favor for someone else.

гҖңгҒӘгӮү means "if" or "in the case of" and often gives advice.
гҖңгҒҹгӮү is a conditional "if/when" that also expresses sequence.
гҖңгҒҫгҒҫ means "as is" or "unchanged".
гҖңгҒ®гҒ§ gives a reason in a polite or softer way than гҒӢгӮү.
гҖңгҒҹгӮҠгҖңгҒҹгӮҠ lists representative actions, like "do things like A and B".
пҪһгҒҠгҒҶпјҸпҪһгӮҲгҒҶ is the volitional form, used to say "let's" or "I will".
"пҪһгҒ«йҒ•гҒ„гҒӘгҒ„" means "must be" or "no doubt."

гҖңгҒ«йҒ•гҒ„гҒӘгҒ„ means "must be" or "no doubt".

"гҖңгҒқгҒҶгҒ " can indicate appearance, like "it looks like".

"гҖңгҒӘгҒҢгӮүгӮӮ" means "even while" or "though" (contrast).
